text.title: Value Proposition Canvas
text.summary: Customer Profile (jobs/pains/gains) and Value Map (pain relievers/gain creators) with FIT scoring and decay rules.

list.item: Customer Jobs: functional (tasks to complete), emotional (feelings sought), social (how they want to appear to others)
Pains: obstacles, frustrations, and risks that block job completion — ranked by intensity
Gains: desired outcomes and benefits — ranked by relevance (essential, expected, desired, unexpected)

rule.statement: Cap at most 2 pains above 7/10 per persona. If more than 2 pains exceed 7/10, the agent has inflated the scores.
rule.rationale: Pain inflation makes every problem look existential; it eliminates the prioritization signal that the canvas is supposed to produce.

rule.statement: Bind pains to interview transcript IDs from user-interviews-methods. Uncited pains decay fastest and are the first to be invalidated by market changes.
rule.rationale: VPC pains shift quarterly in early markets; a 6-month-old canvas without transcript anchors is fiction. Citation enables targeted re-validation of specific pain items.

list.item: Products and Services: what is offered (feature list, not the benefit)
Pain Relievers: how each product/service addresses a specific pain — must map 1:1 to pains in the Customer Profile
Gain Creators: how each product/service enables a specific gain — must map 1:1 to gains in the Customer Profile

rule.statement: FIT is achieved when Pain Relievers address the top-ranked pains and Gain Creators enable the essential gains. Focus on pains rated 8+ and gains labeled "essential" or "expected" — ignore "nice to have."
rule.rationale: Building against low-intensity pains and desired-but-not-essential gains produces features customers acknowledge but do not pay for.

rule.statement: Add a valid_until date (90 days from creation) to every VPC. Expire and force regeneration in CI when the date passes.
rule.rationale: VPC pains and gains shift quarterly in early markets. A 6-month-old canvas built on unvalidated assumptions guides product decisions in the wrong direction.

text.reference: VPC template with customer profile and value map sections
